pi_items.source: Computed from degrees/minutes: 50 + 10/60 = 50.166667 (matches task description '50 degrees 10.00 minutes North')
Computed from degrees/minutes: 50 + 11/60 = 50.183333 (matches task description '50 degrees 11.00 minutes North')
Computed from degrees/minutes: -(1 + 20.20/60) = -1.336667 (matches task description '1 degree 20.20 minutes West')
Computed from degrees/minutes: -(1 + 20.00/60) = -1.333333 (matches task description '1 degree 20.00 minutes West')
Latitude difference: 50.183333 - 50.166667 = 0.016667° = 1.0 minute of arc = 1.0 nautical mile. Verified mathematically: 1 minute of latitude = 1 NM by definition

Task requires '±0.05 NM tolerance'. Conversion: ±0.05 NM = ±0.05 minutes of arc = ±0.05/60 degrees = ±0.000833 degrees. Verifier.py line 109 correctly uses: tolerance = 0.05 / 60.0 = 0.000833. Metadata value 0.001 is inconsistent with both the verifier and the task requirement
Path appears consistent with Bridge Command directory structure documented in setup_bridgecommand.sh which installs Bridge Command to /opt/bridgecommand and expects World/ subdirectory

Path appears consistent with Bridge Command directory structure documented in setup_bridgecommand.sh which expects Scenarios/ subdirectory. Naming convention with 'm) ' prefix matches standard Bridge Command scenario naming

pi_items.verified_value: Open source ship navigation simulator, GPL v2 licensed, written in C++ using Irrlicht engine
pi_items.source: Confirmed via https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc and https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/. GitHub shows active development (v5.10.3 as of September 2025), 1,946 commits, 9 releases

pi_items.metadata_value: Real nautical concept for ship speed calibration
pi_items.verified_value: Measured Miles are real, documented nautical features. Known examples include Stokes Bay (Gosport), Skelmorlie (Scotland), Lower Hope Reach (Thames Estuary)
Coordinates place the location in English Channel off southern England, consistent with Isle of Wight area, but no documented real Measured Mile course found at these specific coordinates
pi_items.source: Web search for specific coordinates and regional Measured Mile locations. Found no documentation of a real course at 50.166667°N, 1.336667°E despite searching multiple sources

privileged_info_summary: Bridge Command is a real, open-source ship navigation simulator (GPL v2, C++, Irrlicht engine) with active development. The task creates a synthetic 'MeasuredMile' world based on real nautical concepts: a 1.0 nautical mile course oriented north-south with coordinates at 50.166667°N-50.183333°N, 1.336667°W-1.333333°W. All coordinate conversions are mathematically correct (50°10'N=50.166667°, etc.). A contradiction exists: metadata specifies tolerance=0.001°, but the task requires ±0.05 NM (=±0.000833°) and the verifier.py implements the correct 0.000833° tolerance. The data is synthetic training scenario data, not extracted from real documented Measured Mile courses, though the concept and general location (English Channel) are geographically plausible.
